mentions.raw_name: The Blind Girl / Blind Beth / Arya Stark / Cat of the Canals / Salty / Squab / Weasel / Arry / Arya Horseface
Nymeria (the night wolf; direwolf, experienced through warging)
The tomcat (at Pynto's / in the rafters)
Jon Snow / The Black Bastard of the Wall (referenced)
Old Nan (referenced — told tales of Hardhome)
Dareon (referenced — singer/Night's Watch deserter she killed)
The dead young man (corpse — handsome, curly hair)
The dead old woman (corpse — died smiling)
Three Lyseni sailors (one old, one young, one ear-less)
The new Mermaid (referenced — thirteen, daughter of Prestayn serving maid)
A woods witch (referenced — told wildlings to go to Hardhome)
Beth (referenced — someone Arya knew at Winterfell)
The Merling Queen (referenced — chose new Mermaid)
A Tyroshi sellsword (referenced — possibly fathered S'vrone's child; killed by S'vrone)
Faceless Men / House of Black and White
Night's Watch (Dareon was a deserter; Jon Snow is "Black Bastard of the Wall")
The Merling Queen's Mermaids (performance troupe or court)
Many-Faced God / Him of Many Faces (Arya serves; "Valar morghulis" / "Valar dohaeris")
R'hllor / Lord of Light (worshipers pray outside red temple; "the night is dark and full of terrors"; great iron braziers)
Cult of Starry Wisdom (acolytes sing to evening stars atop scrying tower)
The Stranger (one of the Seven; dead man found at his feet with dark red candle)
Braavosi (founded by escaped slaves; slave trade forbidden; kindly folk)
Lyseni (white-blond hair, smooth fair skin; "blood of the old Freehold")
Ibbenese (whalers; reek of blood and blubber)
Wildlings / Free Folk (from Westeros; fled to Hardhome after Mance's defeat)
Arya's stick (5 feet, leather-wrapped, navigation and combat tool)
Three hidden knives (boot, sleeve, small of back)
Four golden dragons (Westerosi coins; king's head/dragon)
Dreaming candles (conjure visions of things loved and lost)
Dark red candle (at the Stranger's feet)
Braavosi coins (old friends to Arya's touch)
Ship Elephant (Lyseni ship, may have reached Lys)
The waif's milk/cup (bitter, causes blindness or sight)
Warging / skinchanging (Arya enters Nymeria in wolf dreams; sees through the tomcat's eyes)
The gift of death (from the Many-Faced God; people come to drink from the pool and die)
Face-changing (Faceless Men "change their voices as easily as they did their faces")
Dreaming candles (conjure visions of things loved and lost)
The waif's potion/milk (causes and apparently ends blindness; the final burning cup restores sight)
The lying game (detecting lies by sound and touch)
Battle where the King-Beyond-the-Wall was killed (referenced; wildlings fled afterward)
Serpent boats (Braavosi vessels; polemen work them)
Courtesans of Braavos (girls at Satin Palace dream of becoming them)
